svg.text: 13.14 · Security — defense in depth (non-negotiable for money)
no single failure is a breach · least privilege bounds the blast radius of EVERY compromise · regulated (PCI-DSS)
per-SERVICE accounts (not a shared superuser) · strong auth plugins · rotation · NO app uses root
require_secure_transport · REQUIRE SSL → credentials + data not sniffable on the wire (defeats MITM)
InnoDB tablespace + binlog + BACKUP encryption · column encryption for card/PII (M03) · ⚠ KMS — losing the key = un-restorable (13.5)
GRANT only what's needed: reporting = SELECT (replicas) · transfer svc = INSERT/UPDATE specific tables · NO DROP/SUPER → bounds the blast radius
who did what — for compliance (PCI-DSS/SOX), forensics (what did the attacker touch?), detecting misuse · complements the immutable ledger (M01/1.17)
assume any single control can fail → layer them so the system stays secure anyway · a correct DB that's INSECURE is still a catastrophe
security protects the VALUE the whole platform holds · applied per shard (M11) · the friction is accepted + minimized (hardware accel, automated KMS, IaC grants)
